title: dactylorhiza fuchsii subsp. sooana
intro: Dactylorhiza fuchsii subsp. sooana, also known as the spotted marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Ireland. It is a type of terrestrial orchid that grows in marshy and damp areas. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Irish medicine, where it is used to treat respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Dactylorhiza fuchsii subsp. sooana include infusions and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory ailments.
